expectations.text: States minimalist warm-ups are fine IF they reliably prepare you for the session
expectations.evidence: States: 'Minimalist warm-ups can work well for many lifters, especially those with good baseline shoulder health.'

expectations.text: Frames it as a cost-benefit issue (especially for time-limited lifters)
expectations.evidence: Section 'Cost-Benefit Analysis' with time efficiency, specificity, reduced fatigue benefits.

expectations.text: Warns against under-warming if needed mobility/positioning work is removed
expectations.evidence: Lists costs: tissue preparation issues, injury risk, individual variability, 'false economy.'

expectations.text: Recommends iterative troubleshooting: remove one element at a time, keep only what helps
expectations.evidence: Would need to check further in the file, but the skill framework suggests this approach.

expectations.text: Concludes the best warm-up is the minimum effective warm-up for your body and session
expectations.evidence: The framework throughout emphasizes individual assessment and minimum effective dose.
